title: Preparation of Autoinduction Lysogeny broth (LB) for Protein Expression
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c4o99","text":"Auto-induction LB (ZYM-5052) enables high-level recombinant protein expression in E. coli without manual IPTG induction. This protocol provides a streamlined recipe and culture workflow adapted from Studier (2005) for T7-based systems (e.g., pET vectors in BL21(DE3) and derivatives). Medium is assembled from sterile concentrates: 20× NPS salts, 50× “5052” carbon mix (glycerol, glucose, lactose), 1 mM MgSO₄, and appropriate antibiotic(s). Media used in baffled flasks at ≤20–25% fill to ensure aeration. Glucose supports initial growth and represses expression; as it is exhausted, lactose automatically induces T7 RNA polymerase while glycerol sustains continued growth.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":82,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Expression Lysogeny Broth (LB) 1 L Tryptone 10 g, (1% of Autoinduction final volume) Yeast Extract 5 g, (0.5% of Autoinduction final volume) Ultra-pure water up to 930 mL
Preparation of Expression LB Add all dry ingredients to a 1 L measuring cylinder

After letting all solutions cool to Room temperature : Add to a 5 L baffled flask: 1 mL of 1 Molarity (M) Magnesium Sulphate 1 mL of appropriate antibiotic A B C 1 Stock Concentration Final Concentration 2 Ampicillin 100 mg/mL 100 µg/mL 3 Chloramphenical 25 mg/mL 25 µg/mL 4 Kanamycin 25 mg/mL 25 µg/mL 50 mL of 20x NPS 20 mL of 50x 5052 Solution 1 bottle of Expression LB ( 930 mL )
5052 Solution (50x) 500 mL Glycerol 125 mL, (25%) Glucose 12.5 g, (2.5%) a-Lactose 50 g, (10%) Ultra-pure water up to 500 mL

Nitrogen-Phosphate Salts (NPS; 20x) 1 L Ammonium Sulfate (NH 4 ) 2 SO 4 66.07 g 0.5 Molarity (M) Monopotassium phosphate KH 2 PO 4 174.2 g 1 Molarity (M) Disodium phosphate Na 2 HPO 4 141.96 g 1 Molarity (M) Ultra-pure water up to 1 L
Preparation of NPS (20x) Add dry ingredients to a 1 L measuring cylinder
Add ultra-pure water up to 930 mL
Preparation of 5052 Solution (50x) Add dry ingredients to a 1 L measuring cylinder
Add glycerol to a 500 mL measuring cylinder
Add magnetic stir bar and mix thoroughly
Add mixuture to 1 L Duran bottle, place lid loosely, and autoclave to sterilise with settings: 00:20:00 121 °C
Add ultra-pure water up to 1 L
Add magnetic stir bar and mix thoroughly
Add mixuture to 1 L Duran bottle, place lid loosely, and autoclave to sterilise with settings: 00:20:00 121 °C
Magnesium Sulphate 50 mL 1 Molarity (M) Magnesium Sulphate MgSO 4 12.32 g 1 Molarity (M) Ultra-pure water up to 50 mL
Preparation of Magnesium Sulphate Add magnesium sulphate to a 50 mL measuring cylinder
Add ultra-pure water up to 500 mL
Add mixuture to 500 mL Duran bottle, place lid loosely, and autoclave to sterilise with settings: 00:20:00 121 °C
Add ultra-pure water up to 50 mL
Add magnetic stir bar and mix thoroughly
Add mixuture to 50 mL Duran bottle, place lid loosely, and autoclave to sterilise with settings: 00:20:00 121 °C
Add inoculate 1 mL of starter culture to begin expression in desired conditions
Antibiotics 1 mL of filter-sterilised selected antibiotics at stock concentration. go to step #10 for common stock concentrations.

Alternatively, measure out appropriate volumes of autoinduction media into other sizes of baffled flasks: A B 1 Media Volume (mL) Flask Size (mL) 2 125 500 3 250 1000 4 500 2000 Media to flask ratio generally 1:4
creator: Alexander Tynan
